title: Seeded Simulation, Input Replay, State Hashes, Settings, and Save Migration · Runtime Lens: Schema Migration
summary: Rendering consumes random draws, replay stores key state without timing, floats are compared as formatted text, settings overwrite atomically unsafe, and corrupted saves silently reset. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the schema migration increment in reproducible replay and versioned local state. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.
Validate versioned settings and scores, write atomically, limit size, reject corruption, and retain recovery guidance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-schema-migration — Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-determinism-replay-save-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-determinism-replay-save-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_determinism_replay_save_2(seed: int = 4217, input_frames: int = 3600, replay_frames: int = 3600, state_hash_equal: bool = True, schema_version: int = 2, corrupted_save_rejected: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if seed < 0 or input_frames < 1:
        return False, input_frames, "replay-domain"
    if replay_frames != input_frames:
        return False, replay_frames, "replay-length"
    if not state_hash_equal:
        return False, replay_frames, "simulation-drift"
    if schema_version < 1 or not corrupted_save_rejected:
        return False, schema_version, "save-contract"
    assert replay_frames == input_frames
    return True, schema_version, "reproducible-run"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Schema Migration model · Theory Schema Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Schema Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4562 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Schema Migration model · Theory Schema Migration.
steps.why: Retrieving Schema Migration through the concrete reproducible replay and versioned local state case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Schema Migration model · Theory Schema Migration, keep Schema Migration connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Schema Migration model · Theory Schema Migration, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Schema Migration precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Schema Migration model · Theory Schema Migration, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 4562 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate seeded randomness and input frames from rendering so replay, save migration, and corruption rejection are reproducible; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate supported versions explicitly and preserve backups while rejecting unknown future state without data loss.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Schema Migration model · Theory Schema Migration, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Adding defaults to a dictionary is a complete save migration strategy.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-schema-migration-1vf9jl.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-schema-migration-1vf9jl(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_schema_migration_1vf9jl\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?replay_frames != input_frames)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not state_hash_equal)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?schema_version < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not corrupted_save_rejected)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
